title: Dotcom Idle Game
summary: Build a rich TypeScript incremental web game that starts simple and grows into a deep resource-management and replayability loop with dotcom-bubble satire.
acceptanceCriteria: Ship a coherent, runnable incremental web game rather than a design doc or dashboard shell.
Implement a real progression loop that begins simply and becomes strategically richer over time.
Model both active and passive play in a way that creates meaningful tradeoffs rather than pure click spam.
Include replayability, reset structure, prestige, or a clear changed-run scaffold that alters later strategic texture.
Leave readable docs for development, game design, and balance / progression expectations.

candidates.label: Click to engine
candidates.promptHint: Prioritize a tight early clicker loop that unfolds into production chains, automation, and replayability through one strong shared simulation core.

candidates.label: Market satire management
candidates.promptHint: Prioritize a more management-forward take where resources, valuation, investors, staffing, and automation deepen the same shared idle economy over time.

hiddenChecks.title: Repo text reflects the dotcom satire and idle design

profile.summary: Judge whether the repo feels like the beginning of a genuinely deep incremental game rather than a clicker clone, dashboard shell, or design-doc-heavy prototype.
priorities: Treat hidden checks as the structural floor, not the main meaning of success.
Prefer one deep, evolving idle/progression spine over broad feature sprawl.
Care about active/passive play interplay, pacing, replayability, and balance thinking.
reward: A satisfying early loop that unfolds into richer resource, automation, and replay structures.
Shared simulation/economy systems that multiple UI surfaces depend on.
Thoughtful progression and balance reasoning with explicit milestone expectations for different play styles.
Satirical theme expression that emerges through mechanics and progression, not just flavor text.
penalize: Cookie-clicker shallowness, disconnected minigames, spreadsheet theater without fun, and prestige/replay ideas that are merely sketched but not systemically grounded.
Feature sprawl where many panels exist but the underlying economy and progression remain thin.
Aesthetic flourish that substitutes for real idle/incremental design.
opportunities: Suggest next moves that deepen the shared economy/progression spine, improve pacing, or make active and passive play interact more interestingly.
